description:
## Background

Switching is the must-have multi-workspace behavior, and it is where a process-global cwd or eager UI update would be most harmful. A user must never see Issue Explorer data from Workspace A under Workspace B or lose a good snapshot because a candidate fails. Concurrent selections also need deterministic behavior.

## What to build

Make selecting a known Workspace or choosing another folder an atomic switch. While a candidate validates and loads, retain the old Current Workspace and complete Issue Explorer snapshot. Commit and display the target only after all views and the current/MRU store update succeed. Provide retryable, dismissible feedback for post-validation failures.

## Technical Approach

- Assign every switch request a monotonically increasing generation. A newer selection immediately supersedes an earlier Pending Workspace; stale results must be discarded after each asynchronous boundary and must never persist or publish.
- Keep the target Pending, never Current, through validation and All/Ready/Blocked loading. The selector may identify the pending target, but the visible Current Workspace and Issue Explorer snapshot remain the old ones.
- Validation failures use inline selector feedback. After validation succeeds, retain the target in the catalog even if later loading fails. Load or persistence failure with an old snapshot uses a dismissible banner with retry; it must preserve the old state.
- Treat the final store save of `currentWorkspacePath` and MRU promotion as the commit boundary. If it fails, retain the old Current Workspace and do not publish target data.
- The backend is the final stale-result guard; frontend generation checking is an additional defense for events or responses that arrive out of order.

## Considerations

- Initial startup has no old snapshot; its failed restore remains an empty chooser case owned by the catalog slice.
- Do not add automatic background refresh in this work. Switching performs an immediate full load only.
- Retry must run the same transaction and still respect a later user selection.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Switching from Workspace A to Workspace B leaves A visibly Current until B validates, all Issue Explorer views load, and the store commit succeeds.
- [ ] A validation, loading, or persistence failure keeps A Current and preserves its Issue Explorer snapshot; relevant failures have the specified inline or dismissible retry feedback.
- [ ] A valid target that fails loading remains a retryable known Workspace without MRU promotion.
- [ ] When selections race, only the latest generation can publish or persist; a stale earlier result is silent.
- [ ] Successful switching updates both Current Workspace and MRU order exactly once.

## Test Plan

- Rust state-machine tests for success, validation failure, load failure, persistence failure, retry, and stale-generation outcomes.
- TauRPC/frontend integration tests proving old-snapshot preservation, pending state, banners, retry, and stale-event rejection.
- Real desktop WebDriver coverage with two distinguishable disposable Beadwork repositories: select A, switch to B, and attempt an invalid target while B is Current.
- Run `cargo test`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, TypeScript checking, and the workspace-management desktop suite.

## Transaction and interaction amendments

A Pending Workspace is cancellable. Cancel must invalidate its request generation, clear pending presentation, retain the old Current Workspace snapshot when one exists, and return an initial user with no snapshot to the empty chooser. A newer selection has the same invalidation effect and should take precedence immediately. Where the command layer can stop obsolete read-only child processes safely, it should; correctness never depends on killing them because stale results are rejected by generation.

The visible Workspace transition includes Issue Explorer interaction context. On successful commit, clear the selected Issue and local Issue Search before the target snapshot is interactive; Issue IDs can overlap across repositories and a search query is scoped to its prior Workspace. Preserve the selected Issue List View if it remains a valid lens. Dismissing an error banner hides only session feedback and does not remove the known retryable target.

All pending, retry, cancellation, removal, and final commit operations use the serialized backend state service. Removing a Pending target invalidates it; removing Current while another target is Pending clears Current and invalidates the pending request rather than allowing an automatic replacement.

### Additional acceptance criteria

- [ ] A user can cancel a Pending switch without losing the old Current Workspace snapshot.
- [ ] A successful switch cannot retain selected Issue Detail or Issue Search state from the prior Workspace.
- [ ] A stale, cancelled, or removed target never publishes or persists after its generation is invalidated.
- [ ] Dismissing a failure banner preserves the retryable catalog entry and does not alter Current Workspace.

### Additional test plan

- Test cancel with and without a prior Current Workspace, then deliver the cancelled command result and prove it is ignored.
- Test removal of Pending and Current Workspaces while work is in flight.
- Test overlapping Issue IDs and active search text across two Workspaces to prove detail and search reset.
- Test banner dismissal separately from retry and catalog removal.

comments.text:
## Atomicity rule

The visible Current Workspace and Issue Explorer snapshot are one unit. No target title, path, or issue data may become current until the entire candidate transaction and its durability commit succeed. Generation checks protect this rule when user selections overlap.

Changelog: atomic switching now has additional invariants. A successful switch must clear the selected Issue and local Issue Search before the new snapshot is interactive, while preserving the selected Issue List View when it remains meaningful. The selector must expose Cancel while Pending; cancel increments/invalidate generation, retains the old snapshot, and prevents a late result from publishing. Dismissing a failure banner hides only that session feedback, not the retryable catalog entry. Removal of a Pending or Current Workspace must invalidate pending work through the state service.

Changelog: expanded the task body with cancellation, selection/search reset, banner-dismissal semantics, removal races, and explicit acceptance/tests. These are product-safety requirements, not optional polish.

title: Switch Workspaces atomically and recover from failures
